description: High-power rockets fly at high velocities where the parasitic drag of external protuberances like launch guides becomes severe. Open the 'two_stage_high_power_rocket.ork' design from /home/ga/Documents/rockets/two_stage_high_power_rocket.ork. First, run a baseline simulation of the clean airframe and note the apogee. Second, add exactly two Launch Lug components to the Booster body tube to represent Large Lugs (Length: 50 mm, Outer diameter: 20 mm, Inner diameter: 18 mm), run the simulation, and record the new apogee. Third, modify the two Launch Lugs to represent Small Lugs (Length: 30 mm, Outer diameter: 10 mm, Inner diameter: 8 mm), run the simulation, and record the new apogee. Save the optimized design with the Small Lugs installed to /home/ga/Documents/rockets/guided_rocket.ork. Finally, write an analysis report to /home/ga/Documents/exports/launch_guide_report.txt containing the baseline apogee, the apogee for the Large Lugs configuration, the apogee for the Small Lugs configuration, and a brief recommendation statement confirming the selection of the Small Lugs.
